title: Publish a Container Port
description:
## Expose a Container to the Host

A container's ports are isolated by default. To reach a containerised web server from the host you must **publish** its port.

**Requirements:**
1. Run an **`nginx:alpine`** container named **`web-pub`** in the background
2. Publish the container's port **80** to host port **8080**

> 💡 The publish flag is `-p <host>:<container>`, e.g. `-p 8080:80`. Verify with `docker port web-pub`.

hints.title: Publish a port at run time
hints.body: Combine -d, --name and -p to run detached, named, with port 80 mapped to host 8080.

hints.title: Inspect the published port
hints.body: docker port shows the host mapping for a container port.

validation.description: Checks that 'web-pub' is running with container port 80 published to host port 8080.

commands.description: Container port 80 is published to host port 8080
